content.question: Do church-maintained and minority self-government schools operating selective gimnáziums view continued operation of these tracks as a core institutional autonomy right, or as a contingent operational choice that could be negotiated if broader public equity concerns are framed as binding on all maintainers?
content.reduction_path: Conduct structured interviews with leadership of major church-maintained and minority-school networks to clarify non-negotiable vs. discretionary elements of their selective-track operations and identify possible joint-governance or accountability frameworks that would preserve maintainer autonomy while addressing state equity concerns.
